title: Associate, Corporate Banking
description:
Purpose of the Role

The Associate supports the Corporate Banking (CB) team in delivering high-quality credit, execution, and client support. The role combines analytical responsibilities with corporate banking support, ensuring compliance with internal policies, credit standards, and regulatory requirements.

Accountabilities

  • Champions a customer focused culture to deepen client relationships and leverage broader Bank relationships, systems and knowledge.
  • Understand how the Bank’s risk appetite and risk culture should be considered in day-to-day activities and decisions.
  • Actively pursues effective and efficient operations of his/her respective areas in accordance with Scotiabank’s Values, its Code of Conduct and the Global Sales Principles, while ensuring the adequacy, adherence to and effectiveness of day-to-day business controls to meet obligations with respect to operational, compliance, AML/ATF/sanctions and conduct risk.
  • Champions a high-performance environment and contributes to an inclusive work environment.

Credit Analysis & Execution Support

  • Assist in preparing credit proposals, including financial analysis and risk assessment.
  • Draft supporting documents such as 1035 memos, annual reviews, and renewals.
  • Support drafting of offer letters and loan documentation, coordinating with stakeholders.
  • Monitor compliance with credit conditions and portfolio requirements.

Origination Support

  • Conduct industry and company research for origination and client pitches.
  • Prepare pitch books, meeting materials, and market updates.
  • Support client onboarding and KYC reviews.

Portfolio Monitoring

  • Track client performance and assess ongoing financial/industry developments.
  • Identify early‑warning indicators and escalate issues to senior team members.
  • Risk, Compliance & Conduct
  • Apply AML, KYC, and risk management policies in daily work.
  • Ensure adherence to credit policies, approval standards, and regulatory guidelines.

Skills & Experience Required

  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent professional experience in Finance, accounting, economics or related disciplines.
  • Strong analytical and financial modelling skills.
  • Understanding of corporate financial statements and credit fundamentals.
  • Strong communication and multi‑stakeholder coordination skills.
  • Attention to detail and ability to work under tight deadlines.

Preferred

  • Knowledge of Singapore/APAC markets and banking products (loans, FX, trade, DCM).
  • Experience performing financial analysis, credit assessment, or advisory work within regulated financial or professional services.
  • Korean language capability is an advantage to support communication with Korean speaking stakeholders.

districts.location: D01 Cecil, Marina, People’s Park, Raffles Place
